{{Quote|Kills conduit worms where they live!|Tag line for Cyanoxis D-587|<ref name="Article" />}}
'''Cyanoxis D-587''' was a [[Poison/Legends|poison]] produced by a company called [[Toxico/Legends|Toxico]] that was designed to [[Death/Legends|exterminate]] [[Conduit worm/Legends|conduit worms]], vermin that thrived on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. In [[23 BBY/Legends|23]] and [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], Toxico advertised Cyanoxis D-587 on Coruscant's [[HoloNet News/Legends|HoloNet News]], employing a disclaimer stating that the poison was not intended as a weapon.

==Description==
[[File:ToxicoAd-HNNVol531-46.gif|thumb|right|100px|Cyanoxis D-587 advert in the [[HoloNet News/Legends|HoloNet News]]]]
Cyanoxis D-587 was a [[Poison/Legends|poison]] designed to [[Death/Legends|kill]] [[Conduit worm/Legends|conduit worms]],<ref name="Article" /> annelids that infested [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], feeding off the electrical wiring channels that ran throughout the [[Planet/Legends|planet]].

==History==
A company, [[Toxico/Legends|Toxico]], manufactured Cyanoxis D-587 during the waning [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. In the years [[23 BBY/Legends|23]] and [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], Toxico placed an advertisement for Cyanoxis D-587 in Coruscant's [[HoloNet News/Legends|HoloNet News]]. In the advertisement, Toxico claimed that the poison would eliminate conduit worms in their natural habitat. The company also placed small a disclaimer in their advertisement, stating that Cyanoxis D-587 was not to be used as a weapon.<ref name="Article" />

==Behind the scenes==
An advert for Cyanoxis D-587 was featured in [[HoloNet News (original website)|HoloNet News]], an [[in-universe]] website created in [[2002]] to publicize the upcoming film {{Film|II}}.
